---
title: "Plots"
author: "Author"
date: "2026-05-13"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
```


```{r}
unique <- read.csv("../results/unique_stgm1_results.csv",stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
repro <- read.csv("../results/reproducible_stgm2_results.csv",stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
noncultural <- read.csv("../results/total_stgm3_results.csv",stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
```

```{r}
dim(unique)
dim(repro)
colnames(unique)
colnames(repro)
colnames(noncultural)
```
```{r}
merged <- merge(unique, repro, by = "model_terms", all = TRUE)
```

```{r}
merged1 <- merge(merged, noncultural, by = "model_terms", all = TRUE)
```


```{r}
write.csv(merged1, "../data/cleaned/stergm_results_data.csv")
```

# Plotting marginal effects
```{r}
colnames(merged1)
merged1$model_terms
```
```{r}
custom_order <- c(
  "Baseline Density (Edges)",
  "Reciprocity (Mutuality)",
  "Absolute difference of Δlog GDP per capita",
  "Sum of Δlog GDP per capita",
  "Geographic distance (log)",
  "Contiguity",
  "Common language",
  "Shares common colonizer",
  "Shares colony-colonizer history",
  "Regional Trade Agreement",
  "Both Landlocked Status Match",
  "Developing -> Advanced",
  "Advanced -> Developing",
  "Advanced -> Advanced"
)
```

```{r}
 #2. RESHAPE THE WIDE DATASET INTO LONG FORMAT
long_data_clean <- merged1 %>%
  # Select only the functional plotting columns
  select(model_terms, odds_u, lci_u, uci_u, odds_r, lci_r, uci_r, odds_t, lci_t, uci_t) %>%
  
  # Pivot columns down so product type (_u vs _r) becomes a row grouping variable
  pivot_longer(cols = -model_terms,
    names_to = c(".value", "Product_Type"),
    names_pattern = "(.*)_(u|r|t)") %>%
  # Re-label columns with clean academic English
  mutate(Product_Type = case_when(
    Product_Type == "u" ~ "Unique Cultural Products",
    Product_Type == "r" ~ "Reproducible Cultural Products",
    Product_Type == "t" ~ "Non-Cultural Products"
  )) %>%
  
  # Separate the STERGM structural process prefix (Form vs Persist)
  separate(model_terms, into = c("Model_Type", "Raw_Variable"), sep = "\\(1\\)~") %>%
  mutate(Model_Type = case_when(
    Model_Type == "Form"    ~ "Tie Formation Sub-model",
    Model_Type == "Persist" ~ "Tie Persistence Sub-model"
  )) %>%
  
  # Clean up the variable names matching your custom labels
  mutate(Clean_Variable = case_when(
    Raw_Variable == "edges"                      ~ "Baseline Density (Edges)",
    Raw_Variable == "mutual"                     ~ "Reciprocity (Mutuality)",
    Raw_Variable == "absdiff.D_log_gdp_pc"       ~ "Absolute difference of Δlog GDP per capita",
    Raw_Variable == "nodecov.D_log_gdp_pc"       ~ "Sum of Δlog GDP per capita",
    Raw_Variable == "edgecov.log_dist"           ~ "Geographic distance (log)",
    Raw_Variable == "edgecov.contig_mat"         ~ "Contiguity",
    Raw_Variable == "edgecov.lang_mat"           ~ "Common language",
    Raw_Variable == "edgecov.col_mat"            ~ "Shares common colonizer",
    Raw_Variable == "edgecov.colony"             ~ "Shares colony-colonizer history",
    Raw_Variable == "edgecov.net_dynamic_rta"    ~ "Regional Trade Agreement",
    Raw_Variable == "nodematch.landlocked"       ~ "Both Landlocked Status Match",
    Raw_Variable == "mix.development.0.1"        ~ "Developing -> Advanced",
    Raw_Variable == "mix.development.1.0"        ~ "Advanced -> Developing",
    Raw_Variable == "mix.development.1.1"        ~ "Advanced -> Advanced",
    TRUE ~ Raw_Variable
  )) %>%
  
  # Enforce your custom factor sequence
  mutate(Clean_Variable = factor(Clean_Variable, levels = custom_order))

# 3. DEFINE THE UNIFIED POSITION DODGE
pd <- position_dodge(width = 0.55)

figure3 <- ggplot(long_data_clean,
  aes(x = odds,y = Clean_Variable,color = Product_Type,shape = Product_Type)) +
  # Reference line at OR = 1
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1,linetype = "dashed",color = "gray55",linewidth = 0.7) +
  # Confidence intervals
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lci, xmax = uci),height = 0,linewidth = 1,alpha = 0.9,position = pd) +
  # Points
  geom_point(size = 2.8,stroke = 0.6,position = pd) +
  # Separate formation and persistence
  facet_wrap(~ Model_Type,scales = "free_x") +
#  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 8)) +
  # COLORS
  scale_color_manual(name = "Product Classification:",
    breaks = c("Unique Cultural Products","Reproducible Cultural Products","Non-Cultural Products"),
    values = c("Unique Cultural Products"       = "#1F4E79","Reproducible Cultural Products" = "#74a55f","Non-Cultural Products"= "#B8812A")) +
  # SHAPES
  scale_shape_manual(name = "Product Classification:",
    breaks = c(
      "Unique Cultural Products",
      "Reproducible Cultural Products",
      "Non-Cultural Products"),
    values = c(
      "Unique Cultural Products"       = 16, # circle
      "Reproducible Cultural Products" = 17, # triangle
      "Non-Cultural Products"          = 18 )) + # diamond 

  # FORCE MERGED LEGEND
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3)),
    shape = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3))) +
  labs(x = "Odds Ratio (95% CI)",y = NULL) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11,colour = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11,colour = "black"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold",size = 11),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold",size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold",size = 10),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray96",color = "gray80"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray92",linewidth = 0.45),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray94",linewidth = 0.35),
    legend.box = "horizontal")
```



```{r}
ggsave("../results/Fig3.tiff", plot = figure3, device = "tiff", width = 15, height = 9, dpi = 800, bg = "white")
```
